title: BigDecimal and Rational thread explicit allocators — inconsistent with Zinc's ambient allocator design
description: The ambient allocator feature exists specifically to remove explicit allocator threading. But BigDecimal and Rational examples show: 'var x = try BigDecimal.parse(str, allocator)' and 'var y = try one_third.add(one_third, allocator)'. These pass allocators explicitly, the old Zig way. Meanwhile the spec states 'Both types use using(allocator) naturally' — which is not demonstrated by the code examples. If these types really use using(allocator), the examples are wrong. If they don't, then every BigDecimal/Rational operation requires explicit allocator threading, which is exactly the problem Zinc claims to solve.
